parsed_review.summary: CP00-469 is a descriptor-only RP15 analytics-core governance pack (risk C, 150 units, RP15.P06.M06.S09-RP15.P07.M03.S10) that closes RP15.P06 (M06 tail through M10) and opens the RP15.P07 foundation (M00-M03 head). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack's claims are supported. (1) Scope/coverage is internally consistent across the baseline plan pack, manifest, registry binding/requirements (registry.js:273,1571), contract projection, unit tests (model.test.js:1113-1169), and the validation script: deliverable counts sum to 150 (36+17+4+26+18+43+4+2), phase counts (P06=89,P07=61) and the nine micro-phase row counts (14+22+22+20+11+11+20+20+10) reconcile, and M06 correctly begins at S09 since S01-S08 belonged to CP00-468. (2) No-write/no-real-data/descriptor-only boundaries are enforced in freezeCp469Result (service.js:2046), the row factory (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false), and the contract no_write_attestation, and are independently asserted by both the unit test and validate-rp15-analytics-core-contract.mjs. (3) Authority boundary holds: review-request.json declares read_only with tools Read/Grep/Glob; descriptor.authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false; no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false; human final approval required for runtime opening (CP00-470). (4) Hermes is used only as a deterministic H15 review/evidence descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false; receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write), not product runtime. (5) CP178+ review hardening worked: the terminated attempt-01 (status 143, 0-byte stdout/stderr) was quarantined under invalid_attempts_preserved and not used as evidence, the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON, and forbidden_review_evidence (registry.js:600) encodes every required blocker (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, missing references, claude_final_approval_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim). (6) The pack-level production_ready flag is intentionally false (status implemented_pending_review); it asserts only implementation/plan gate passage pending final closeout, which the consistent implementation and recorded exit-0 command evidence support, and the post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent until this review is normalized. No P0/P1/P2 findings; two non-blocking P3 transparency notes. Verification is source-inspection based per the read-only review mode; final enterprise-trust closure remains a human approval step outside this receipt.

findings.title: Verification is source-inspection only; validation commands not independently re-executed
findings.details: As a read-only reviewer (no Bash), I could not independently re-run the validation commands (npm test, npm run validate, rpXX:*:validate, etc.). The pack records exit_code 0 for all pre-review gates in command-evidence.json, and I verified the underlying source (registry/service/validators/contract/test/script) is mutually consistent and that the validator and unit tests genuinely assert the descriptor-only, coverage, and authority-boundary invariants. This is the intended review modality (source_inspection_mode: read_tools_used) rather than a defect.
findings.recommendation: No action required. The post-review gated commands (closeout-pack:validate, review receipt validation, git diff --check --cached) must still be executed and recorded before commit, as already noted in command-evidence.json.

findings.title: index.js listed as active implementation ref but unmodified
findings.details: changed-file-scope.json lists packages/analytics/src/index.js among active_pack_implementation_refs, but it does not appear in git_status_rows as modified. Confirmed benign: index.js is a wildcard re-export (export * from registry/service/validators), so the new CP469 exports are surfaced without editing index.js. Logical-scope membership without a dirty-file entry is expected here.
findings.recommendation: No action required; the wildcard re-export correctly exposes the CP469 public exports listed in required_public_exports.
